dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8147 IH: Prioritizing Rural Broadband Affordability Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. McClain Delaney (for herself and Mr. Bresnahan) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Agriculture, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To require service affordability to be considered in awarding grants under rural broadband programs administered by the Department of Agriculture.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Prioritizing Rural Broadband Affordability Act.
Section 601 of the Rural Electrification Act of 1936 (7 U.S.C. 950bb) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subsection (c)(2), by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: In determining whether a household is unserved for purposes of this section, the Secretary shall consider the affordability of broadband service.

paragraph.text: in subsection (d)(2), by striking subparagraph (C) and inserting the following:

subparagraph.text: In determining whether a proposed service territory is unserved for purposes of subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall consider the affordability of broadband service in the service territory.

section.text: The amendments made by this Act shall take effect on the date that is 1 year after the date of the enactment of this Act.
